draft.introduction: Revelation 2:1–29 contains the letters to the churches of Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, and Thyatira. Each letter follows a pattern: commendation, critique, and a promise “to the one who conquers.” From Codex Sinaiticus.

translation.text: To the angel of the church in Ephesus write: The words of him who holds the seven stars in his right hand, who walks among the seven golden lampstands. I know your works, your toil and your patient endurance. But I have this against you, that you have abandoned the love you had at first. Remember therefore from where you have fallen; repent, and do the works you did at first.
translation.original_text: τω αγγελω της εν εφεσω εκκλησιας γραψον

translation.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers I will grant to eat of the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God.
translation.original_text: ο εχων ους ακουσατω τι το πνευμα λεγει ταις εκκλησιαις

translator_notes.note: The promise of access to the “tree of life” (το ξυλον της ζωης) reverses the expulsion from Eden in Genesis 3:22–24. What was lost at the beginning of the biblical story is restored at its end. The pattern of fall and restoration frames the entire scriptural narrative.
